beats.prompt: By now you've worked these out the long way. Match each pattern to its wait.

feedback.correct: All four — now let's get them in seconds instead of a linear system.
hints: Tap a pattern, then its wait.

beats.prompt: Tap each row to slide HH over itself and check for a match where the start reappears at the end. Each overlap of length L is worth 2^L flips (2 to the power L).
beats.interviewNote: These overlap lengths are the pattern's correlation/border set (Guibas–Odlyzko); in binary the Conway leading number encodes the same thing.

feedback.correct: HH overlaps at lengths 1 and 2 — worth 2 and 4.
hints: HH matches at length 2 (itself) and length 1 (the H).

beats.prompt: Quick refresher on powers of two — read the card, then continue.

interaction.title: Powers of two
interaction.body: 2, 4, 8, 16, 32 — each is double the one before. 2^L just means "multiply 2 by itself L times." That's every chip we're about to add.

feedback.correct: Doubling each step — that's all 2^L is.

beats.prompt: Tap each overlap tile to add its 2^L value and total HH's wait.

feedback.correct: 4 + 2 = 6 — that's E[HH], straight off the overlaps. That sum is Σ2^L.

beats.prompt: Press Run to stream many games and watch money-in (the average wait) converge to money-out (the surviving parlays). That convergence is why Σ2^L works.

hero.structuralReadout: Average money in settles on the money out, Σ2^L = 6, for HH.

beats.interviewNote: This is the martingale / optional-stopping argument (Li 1980, ABRACADABRA): a fair game has expected net zero, so E[T] = expected payout = Σ2^L.

feedback.correct: Money in = money out in a fair game, so the wait equals Σ2^L.
hints: The average wait (money in) drifts toward the fixed payout (money out).

beats.prompt: Now apply the shortcut to THH. Each tile is one of its overlaps — tap each to sum THH's wait.

feedback.correct: THH overlaps only at length 3 → 2³ = 8. Done in seconds.
hints: Where does THH's start reappear at its end?

beats.prompt: Apply the shortcut to HTH. Tap each overlap tile — including the sneaky length-1 overlap — to total HTH's wait.

feedback.correct: HTH overlaps at lengths 1 and 3 → 2 + 8 = 10. The length-1 overlap is the trap.
hints: HTH ends in H, and it starts with H…

beats.prompt: Tap each lens card to reveal how the recurrence, the martingale, and the simulation each arrive at the same answer for HH.

lenses.label: Recurrence
lenses.body: Solve E₀ = 1 + ½·E₁ + ½·E₀, E₁ = 1 + ½·E₂ + ½·E₀.

lenses.label: Simulation
lenses.body: The empirical mean of many runs settles here.

feedback.correct: All three land on 6 — the long way, the shortcut, and the experiment agree.

beats.prompt: Reveal the recap cards to see one number confirmed three ways — the course capstone.

feedback.correct: Read the wait straight off the overlaps — and you know why it works.
